chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْ شَهَادَةِ النِّسَاءِ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – What is allowed from the testimony of the women, and what is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/242)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/242)
text: أُجِيزُ شَهَادَةَ النِّسَاءِ فِي الْغُلَامِ صَاحَ أَمْ لَمْ يَصِحْ وَ فِي كُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ لَا يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِ الرِّجَالُ تَجُوزُ شَهَادَةُ النِّسَاءِ فِيهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Abu Nasr, from Dawood Bin Sirhan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> do allow the testimony of the woman regarding the (new-born) boy whether he cried or not cried, and regarding everything which the men cannot look at, the testimony is allowed with regards to it’.
en.sarwar: 13. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn abu Nasr from Dawud ibn Sarhan who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, T accept testimony of women about a boy who cried or did not cry, and about everything to which men cannot look, the testimony of women is acceptable.’”
